description:
You are acting as a monitoring-specialist who has just received a snapshot of service-health metrics in CSV form together with the company-wide alert thresholds in JSON form.  
    Your job is to 1) scan the CSV, 2) raise per-metric alerts whenever any value crosses the corresponding threshold, and 3) write two strictly-formatted artefacts that our automated alerting pipeline will later ingest.

    Directory layout that already exists when you start
    ----------------------------------------------------
    /home/user/monitoring/  
    ├── raw_metrics.csv          (input – do not rename)  
    └── thresholds.json          (input – do not rename)

    • The CSV has the exact header:
          timestamp,hostname,cpu_usage,mem_usage,disk_free

    • The JSON file stores one integer threshold per metric (keys are identical to the CSV header names except for timestamp/hostname).

    What you must produce
    ---------------------
    1. /home/user/monitoring/alerts.log  
       • A plain-text **newline-delimited JSON** (NDJSON) file.  
       • Every line is a single JSON object with the five keys in the exact order below:  
         1) timestamp  (string, ISO-8601 as in the CSV)  
         2) hostname   (string)  
         3) metric     (string, one of cpu_usage | mem_usage | disk_free)  
         4) value      (number, taken verbatim from the CSV row)  
         5) threshold  (number, taken from thresholds.json)  
       • A line is written only if the value breaches the rule:
             cpu_usage  > cpu_usage threshold
             mem_usage  > mem_usage threshold
             disk_free  < disk_free threshold
       • Lines MUST be emitted in the same order you encounter breaches when
         you scan raw_metrics.csv **top-to-bottom** and evaluate the three
         metrics left-to-right (cpu_usage → mem_usage → disk_free).
       • No extra whitespace before/after any line.  
       • Example line shape (values will differ):
           {"timestamp":"2023-08-01T10:05:00Z","hostname":"db01","metric":"cpu_usage","value":85,"threshold":80}

    2. /home/user/monitoring/alert_summary.csv  
       • A comma-separated file (no spaces) with header:  
           hostname,metric,alert_count  
       • Each subsequent row shows how many alerts a (hostname,metric) pair
         accumulated in alerts.log.  
       • Sort rows primarily by hostname (ascending, ASCII), secondarily by
         metric name (ascending).  
       • Example shape with made-up counts:  
           hostname,metric,alert_count  
           db01,cpu_usage,3  
           db01,disk_free,1  
           web01,cpu_usage,2  
       • The file MUST end with a single trailing newline (POSIX style).

    Validation hints
    ----------------
    • alerts.log must contain **exactly** the lines that correspond to breached
      values—no duplicates and no missed breaches.  
    • alert_summary.csv must be internally consistent with alerts.log; the
      judge recomputes the counts.  
    • Keep original input files unchanged.  
    • You do NOT need root and should not use sudo.

    When you are done you should have these four files inside
    /home/user/monitoring/ (two were pre-existing, two created by you):
       raw_metrics.csv      [unchanged input]
       thresholds.json      [unchanged input]
       alerts.log           [generated by you]
       alert_summary.csv    [generated by you]

    Produce no additional output files.
truth:
<!--
    The two input files already present for the agent.
    -->
    <preexisting_files>
        <file path="/home/user/monitoring/raw_metrics.csv"><![CDATA[
timestamp,hostname,cpu_usage,mem_usage,disk_free
2023-08-01T10:00:00Z,web01,45,60,40
2023-08-01T10:05:00Z,db01,85,70,25
2023-08-01T10:10:00Z,web01,90,80,14
2023-08-01T10:15:00Z,web02,70,78,12
2023-08-01T10:20:00Z,db01,65,74,30
2023-08-01T10:25:00Z,web01,55,65,50
2023-08-01T10:30:00Z,web02,82,60,20
2023-08-01T10:35:00Z,db01,75,76,10
2023-08-01T10:40:00Z,web01,60,70,18
2023-08-01T10:45:00Z,web01,88,77,13
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/monitoring/thresholds.json"><![CDATA[
{
  "cpu_usage": 80,
  "mem_usage": 75,
  "disk_free": 15
}
]]></file>
    </preexisting_files>

    <!--
    The exact files and contents the judge will look for after
    the agent finishes.
    -->
    <expected_output_files>

        <file path="/home/user/monitoring/alerts.log"><![CDATA[
{"timestamp":"2023-08-01T10:05:00Z","hostname":"db01","metric":"cpu_usage","value":85,"threshold":80}
{"timestamp":"2023-08-01T10:10:00Z","hostname":"web01","metric":"cpu_usage","value":90,"threshold":80}
{"timestamp":"2023-08-01T10:10:00Z","hostname":"web01","metric":"mem_usage","value":80,"threshold":75}
{"timestamp":"2023-08-01T10:10:00Z","hostname":"web01","metric":"disk_free","value":14,"threshold":15}
{"timestamp":"2023-08-01T10:15:00Z","hostname":"web02","metric":"mem_usage","value":78,"threshold":75}
{"timestamp":"2023-08-01T10:15:00Z","hostname":"web02","metric":"disk_free","value":12,"threshold":15}
{"timestamp":"2023-08-01T10:30:00Z","hostname":"web02","metric":"cpu_usage","value":82,"threshold":80}
{"timestamp":"2023-08-01T10:35:00Z","hostname":"db01","metric":"mem_usage","value":76,"threshold":75}
{"timestamp":"2023-08-01T10:35:00Z","hostname":"db01","metric":"disk_free","value":10,"threshold":15}
{"timestamp":"2023-08-01T10:45:00Z","hostname":"web01","metric":"cpu_usage","value":88,"threshold":80}
{"timestamp":"2023-08-01T10:45:00Z","hostname":"web01","metric":"mem_usage","value":77,"threshold":75}
{"timestamp":"2023-08-01T10:45:00Z","hostname":"web01","metric":"disk_free","value":13,"threshold":15}
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/monitoring/alert_summary.csv"><![CDATA[
hostname,metric,alert_count
db01,cpu_usage,1
db01,disk_free,1
db01,mem_usage,1
web01,cpu_usage,2
web01,disk_free,2
web01,mem_usage,2
web02,cpu_usage,1
web02,disk_free,1
web02,mem_usage,1
]]></file>

    </expected_output_files>
